untranslatable_reason: This signal requires state management and multi-day swing classification logic that cannot be expressed as a single-bar condition set. The rule requires tracking established swing direction, handling inside/outside days, and conditional ignoring of price extremes based on prior bar relationships and directional memory. Standard backtest engines operate on per-bar conditions without swing-state tracking.
translation_notes: KAUFMAN's swing classification is a sequential pattern-recognition system requiring: (1) memory of established direction, (2) conditional logic that ignores specific price extremes based on swing type, (3) special handling for inside/outside days. While the entry conditions can be partially expressed (higher high + higher low = upswing start; lower low without higher high = downswing start), the full logic—especially continuation rules and inside-day skipping—requires a custom indicator or state machine. Recommend implementing as a custom swing_direction indicator (returning +1 for upswing, -1 for downswing) rather than as primitive conditions.
